case.caption: Wells v. Drayton Holdings, Inc.
case.court: U.S. District Court, Eastern District of Pennsylvania
claims: Wrongful termination in violation of public policy
Breach of implied contract (progressive discipline policy)
case.disposition: Summary judgment DENIED as to FLSA retaliation and wrongful termination; GRANTED as to breach of implied contract.

appeal_focus_note: User flagged 'cure-period' as the key appellate issue. The opinion does not use the phrase 'cure period.' The closest analogs are (a) the PIP-to-termination window (Oct. 14, 2022 PIP citing 'failure to satisfy PIP milestones' -> Dec. 19, 2022 termination — roughly 66 days), and (b) the disputed protected-activity-to-adverse-action gap that drives the temporal-proximity analysis. Events relevant to both are tagged 'cure_period_relevant: true' below.

events.title: Wells hired by Drayton
events.description: Janet Wells hired as Senior Operations Analyst; reported to Operations Director Michael Chu.
events.source: Compl. ¶ 12; Wells Dep. 18:5-22; Wells Decl. ¶ 3 (Ex. P-1)

events.title: Period of disputed overtime hours
events.description: Wells says she averaged ~53 hrs/week Feb–Oct 2022 covering work of two departed analysts. Drayton timekeeping records show 40–44 hrs/week for the same period. Court did not resolve the discrepancy.

dispute.defendant_position: 40–44 hours/week per timekeeping records (Chu Decl. ¶ 14, Ex. D-3)

events.title: Two client complaints received (per Drayton)
events.description: Drayton points to two client complaints in July and August 2022 as the basis for the later PIP. Wells contends the PIP was pretextual.
events.cure_period_note: Defendant's asserted predicate for the PIP — relevant to whether the PIP/cure was bona fide or pretextual.

events.title: Wells emails Chu about uncompensated overtime (protected activity)
events.description: Wells sent an email to Chu raising concerns about uncompensated overtime, attaching a spreadsheet of her hours worked.
events.cure_period_note: Start of the temporal-proximity window on the plaintiff's theory.

events.title: HR receipt of overtime complaint — DISPUTED DATE
events.description: Wells contends Chu forwarded the email to HR Director Elaine Morris the same day, Sept. 8, 2022.

dispute.plaintiff_position: HR received complaint on Sept. 8, 2022 (Wells Dep. 42:18-43:7).
dispute.defendant_position: HR (Morris) did not receive any forwarded message until Oct. 3, 2022 — see event F06.
dispute.court_treatment: Court found this dispute material to the temporal-proximity element; did not resolve it on summary judgment.

events.cure_period_note: Drives the protected-activity-to-adverse-action gap. Plaintiff's date yields ~5 weeks to PIP; defendant's date yields 11 days.

events.title: HR receipt of overtime complaint — defendant's version
events.description: Morris testified she did not learn of the complaint until Oct. 3, 2022, when Chu first raised it in a meeting.

dispute.plaintiff_position: See event F05 — plaintiff says HR received it Sept. 8, 2022.
dispute.defendant_position: Oct. 3, 2022 first notice to HR (Morris Dep. 31:4-19; Ex. D-7, Morris's calendar).

events.cure_period_note: On defendant's timeline, only 11 days separate HR notice from the PIP — court called this 'even more probative' of causation.

events.title: Wells placed on Performance Improvement Plan (PIP)
events.description: PIP cited alleged deficiencies in client communication. Wells testified her 2021 review was 'exceeds expectations' and that the PIP was pretextual; Drayton tied PIP to the July/Aug. 2022 client complaints (F03).
events.source: Ex. P-9; Wells Dep. 67:8-21; Ex. P-2 (2021 review); Chu Decl. ¶¶ 22-25
events.cure_period_note: Adverse employment action #1 and start of the de facto PIP 'cure' window before termination.

events.title: Drayton terminates Wells's employment
events.description: Termination letter cites 'failure to satisfy PIP milestones.' 66 days after the PIP was issued.
events.cure_period_note: End of the PIP-to-termination 'cure' window. Stated reason for termination directly invokes PIP milestones.

events.title: Complaint filed
events.description: Wells filed Complaint asserting (1) FLSA retaliation, 29 U.S.C. § 215(a)(3); (2) wrongful termination in violation of public policy; (3) breach of implied contract based on Drayton's progressive discipline policy.

events.title: Scheduling order entered
events.description: Court entered scheduling order; fact discovery set to close April 1, 2024.

events.title: Stipulated extension of discovery deadline
events.description: Parties stipulated to extend fact discovery deadline to May 31, 2024. Discovery ultimately included 12 depositions and ~8,400 pages of documents.

events.title: Original fact discovery deadline (superseded)
events.description: Original close-of-fact-discovery date set in the June 30, 2023 scheduling order; superseded by stipulation P04.

events.title: Drayton files Motion for Summary Judgment
Wells files opposition to summary judgment
Drayton files reply brief
events.description: Defendant's reply in support of summary judgment.

events.title: Memorandum Opinion issued
events.description: Court DENIES summary judgment as to FLSA retaliation (and wrongful-termination claim proceeds); GRANTS summary judgment as to breach of implied contract. Retaliation and wrongful-termination claims will proceed to trial.

date_disputes_summary.dispute: When HR received Wells's overtime complaint
date_disputes_summary.appellate_significance: Sets the protected-activity-to-adverse-action gap. Plaintiff's date: ~5 weeks to the PIP; defendant's date: 11 days. Court held a reasonable jury could find temporal proximity under either version (citing Williams v. Phila. Hous. Auth., 380 F.3d 751, 760 (3d Cir. 2004)).
date_disputes_summary.court_resolution: Unresolved — treated as a material factual dispute precluding summary judgment.

date_disputes_summary.appellate_significance: Goes to the underlying FLSA overtime grievance and the bona fides of Wells's protected activity, though not directly to the retaliation timing question.

key_intervals.label: Protected activity (Wells->Chu email) to PIP
key_intervals.note: Plaintiff's framing of temporal proximity (~5 weeks).

key_intervals.label: HR notice (defendant's date) to PIP
key_intervals.note: Defendant's date for HR knowledge; court called this 'even more probative.'

key_intervals.label: PIP to termination ('cure' window)
key_intervals.note: Adverse-action window most directly analogous to a contractual cure period; termination letter cited 'failure to satisfy PIP milestones.'

key_intervals.label: Complaint to Memorandum Opinion
key_intervals.note: Overall pretrial duration through summary judgment ruling.
